seoTitle: How to Create Card Templates in Trello
metaDescription: Turn any Trello card into a reusable template with preset checklists, labels, and descriptions, so you can spawn new tasks instantly.
intro: Rebuilding the same onboarding checklist or client brief from scratch every time wastes time you don't need to spend. This guide covers converting a standard card into a master template so you can spawn fully populated cards with a single click.

steps.heading: Build out a sample card
steps.body: Create a card containing the checklist, description, labels, and member assignments you want to reuse.

steps.heading: Convert it to a template
steps.body: Open the card's menu and toggle the option to mark it as a master template.

steps.heading: Organize your template tray
steps.body: Store your templates so they're easy to find without cluttering your active workflow lists.

steps.heading: Spawn a new card from the template
steps.body: Use the template tray to create a fresh, fully populated card in a single click.

steps.heading: Update templates as needed
steps.body: Modify or remove a master template without affecting any previously generated cards.

steps.heading: Label templates for clarity
steps.body: Apply a high-contrast label to visually separate template cards from your standard, active assignments.

faq.question: Will editing a template change cards I already created from it?
faq.answer: No, modifying or removing a master template does not affect previously generated active project tasks.

faq.question: Where do template cards live on my board?
faq.answer: They're stored in a dedicated template tray so they don't clutter your active workflow lists.

faq.question: What should a good template include?
faq.answer: A balanced template usually contains a preset checklist, a description guideline, and core labels relevant to the recurring task.

faq.question: How do I tell a template card apart from a real task?
faq.answer: Applying a distinct high-contrast label to your templates makes them easy to visually differentiate from active cards.
